To add one of your account's internal parties to the document, send the `account_party_uuid` and `reference`

To add an external party to the document, send these fields:

- `reference`

- `entity_name`

data.current_description:
Lists available [metadata](/#metadata) fields that help you enrich your [documents](/#documents).

## Metadata Types

Each metadata has an associated `type` that defines where its value comes from and what actions can be taken on
it.

| Type | Description |
|------|------------ |
| `account` | Custom metadata fields that are specific to your account. |
| `system_reference` | Pre-defined useful metadata fields provided by fynk.  |
| `system_autofilled` | Fields that are automatically filled by the system in response to events, like "Signing completion date". |
| `system_computed` | Fields that are calculated by the system based on other metadata values. e.g. "Signing duration" is the difference between "Signing date" and "Signed date"
| `system` | Other system-provided metadata fields. |

Note that the `type` is distinct from the `value_type`, which controls which kinds of values may be stored in the
field.
data.summary_suggestion: List available metadata fields for documents

data.current_description:
Updates the value of a [dynamic field](/#dynamic-fields) for a [document](/#documents).

## Value format

The content of the `value` parameter depends on the `type` of the dynamic field. The table below lists the
available types and shows how values of each type should be sent.

| Descriptive name | API `type` | Example values | Description |
|------------------|------------|----------------|-------------|
| Email | `email` | `"user@example.com"` | An email address. |
| Number | `number` | `1234.56` | A numeric value. |
| Currency | `currency` | `"EUR;1234.56"`, `"EUR;1234"` | A string containing a currency code and numeric value concatenated with a semicolon. |
| Currency over time | `currency_duration` | `"EUR;1234.56;monthly"`, `"EUR;123456;yearly"` | A string containing a currency code, numeric value and period (one of `monthly` or `yearly`) concatenated with a semicolon. |
| Select | `select` | `"Blue"` | One of the values from the field's `select_values` list. |
| Date | `date` | `"2025-12-31"` | A date in the format "YYYY-MM-DD". |
| Yes / No | `bool` | `true`, `false` | A boolean value. |
| Duration | `duration` | `"P6D"` (6 days), `"P2W"` (2 weeks), `"PT12H"` (12 hours) | A string containing a [duration specification](https://www.php.net/manual/en/dateinterval.construct.php). |
| Text | `text` | `"Vienna"` | Up to 4096 characters of text. |
| Multi-line text | `textarea` | `"Another text value"` | Up to 4096 characters of text. |
data.description_suggestion: Updates the value of a specific [dynamic field](/#dynamic-fields) within a [document](/#documents). The `value` parameter format depends on the field's type (text, date, currency, select, bool, duration, email, number, textarea, or currency_duration). Returns the updated dynamic field resource. Requires the document UUID and dynamic field UUID in the URL path.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic. Description is comprehensive but could be more concise for AI agents. Added clarity about required parameters in URL path and return value. Consolidated the value format table reference into a more actionable statement about type-dependent formatting.

data.current_summary: Store a file in a document's file storage
data.current_description:
Associates a previously uploaded file with the [document](/#documents) referred to by the URL.

This endpoint requires a three-step upload process:

1. Generate upload URL: Call the [Create document file storage upload URL](/operations/v1.file-uploads.document-file-storage.create) endpoint to get a presigned upload URL
2. Upload file: Make a PUT request to the returned URL with the file contents and the specified headers
3. Associate uploaded file with document: Call this endpoint with `file_upload_uuid` set to the `uuid` received in step 1

The file will be available in the file storage of the document referred to in the URL.

For a complete guide with examples, see [How to store a file in a document's file storage](/#how-to-store-a-file-in-a-documents-file-storage).
data.description_suggestion: Associates a previously uploaded file with a [document](/#documents) by linking it to the document's file storage. This is the final step of a three-step upload process: (1) generate presigned upload URL via [Create document file storage upload URL](/operations/v1.file-uploads.document-file-storage.create), (2) upload file contents to the returned URL, (3) call this endpoint with the `file_upload_uuid` from step 1 and a `file_name`. Returns the stored file resource with UUID, file name, size, and MIME type. Requires document UUID in URL path.

data.current_description:
Adds a [metadata](/#metadata) field to a [document](/#documents) and optionally sets its value.

## Value format

 The content of the `value` parameter depends on the `value_type` of the metadata. The table below lists the
 available types and shows how values of each type should be sent.

 | Descriptive name | API `type` | Example values | Description |
 |------------------|------------|----------------|-------------|
 | Email | `email` | `"user@example.com"` | An email address. |
 | Number | `number` | `1234.56` | A numeric value. |
 | Currency | `currency` | `"EUR;1234.56"`, `"EUR;1234"` | A string containing a currency code and numeric value concatenated with a semicolon. |
 | Currency over time | `currency_duration` | `"EUR;1234.56;monthly"`, `"EUR;123456;yearly"` | A string containing a currency code, numeric value and period (one of `monthly` or `yearly`) concatenated with a semicolon. |
 | Select | `select` | `"Blue"` | One of the values from the field's `select_values` list. |
 | Date | `date` | `"2025-12-31"` | A date in the format "YYYY-MM-DD". |
 | Yes / No | `bool` | `true`, `false` | A boolean value. |
 | Duration | `duration` | `"P6D"` (6 days), `"P2W"` (2 weeks), `"PT12H"` (12 hours) | A string containing a [duration specification](https://www.php.net/manual/en/dateinterval.construct.php). |
 | Text | `text` | `"Vienna"` | Up to 4096 characters of text. |
 | Multi-line text | `textarea` | `"Dublin"` | Up to 4096 characters of text. |
 | Timestamp | `timestamp` | `"2025-06-02T14:30:00+00:00"` | A date/time value. |
 | Clause | `clause` | `true`, `false` | Indicates presence of a particular clause in the document. |

data.current_description:
Updates the details of a [signatory](/#signatories) on a [document](/#documents).

> **Note:** When updating a signatory who has an existing user in the current fynk account

data.description_suggestion: Updates the details of a signatory on a document. For signatories with existing fynk account users (has_account_user is true), only party_uuid and mobile_phone can be modified; attempting to update first_name, last_name, or title will result in a validation error. For signatories without account users, all fields can be updated. Returns the updated signatory object.

data.current_description:
Moves a [document](/#documents) to the review stage in the document workflow.

Requires that the document's `stage` is currently one of: `draft`, `approved_draft`, `approved_review`, `signing`
or `done`, and that there are no [checkpoints](https://help.fynk.com/en/articles/136638-checkpoints-in-fynk)
that would block the transition.

Note that if the document is in the process of being signed, or has been signed already, moving back to review
will cancel the current signing process and invalidate any existing signatures.
data.description_suggestion: Moves a document to the review stage in the document workflow. Requires the document's current stage to be one of: draft, approved_draft, approved_review, signing, or done, and no blocking checkpoints. Returns the updated document with stage set to 'review'. Important side effects: if the document is currently signing or already signed, this transition cancels the signing process and invalidates all existing signatures. Useful for sharing documents with collaborators for feedback before signing.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks explicit information about the return value (updated document object) and doesn't clearly articulate the critical side effect about signature invalidation as a consequence of the state transition. data.current_description:
Adds a [party](/#parties) to a [document](/#documents).

## Adding an internal party

To add one of your account's internal parties to the document, send the `account_party_uuid` and `reference`
fields. The rest of the party data will be copied from the respective "account party".

## Adding an external party

To add an external party to the document, send these fields:

- `reference`
- `entity_type`
- `entity_name`
- `address`
- `scope`

> **Note:** When adding a party that is linked to an account party, only `reference` can be provided. Attempting
> to set the other fields will cause the request to be rejected with a validation error.
data.summary_suggestion: Add internal or external party to document
data.description_suggestion: Adds a party to a document. Supports two workflows: (1) Adding an internal party from your account by providing `account_party_uuid` and `reference` - other party data is copied from the account party; (2) Adding an external party by providing `reference`, `entity_type`, `entity_name`, `address`, and `scope`. Returns the newly created party resource. Note: parties linked to account parties can only have their `reference` field modified; attempting to set other fields will result in a validation error.

data.current_description:
Updates the value of a [dynamic field](/#dynamic-fields) for a [document](/#documents).

## Value format

The content of the `value` parameter depends on the `type` of the dynamic field. The table below lists the
available types and shows how values of each type should be sent.

| Descriptive name | API `type` | Example values | Description |
|------------------|------------|----------------|-------------|

data.current_description:
Associates a previously uploaded file with the [document](/#documents) referred to by the URL.

This endpoint requires a three-step upload process:

The file will be available in the file storage of the document referred to in the URL.

data.current_description:
Lists available [metadata](/#metadata) fields that help you enrich your [documents](/#documents).

## Metadata Types

Each metadata has an associated `type` that defines where its value comes from and what actions can be taken on
it.

| Type | Description |
|------|------------ |
| `account` | Custom metadata fields that are specific to your account. |
| `system_reference` | Pre-defined useful metadata fields provided by fynk.  |
| `system_autofilled` | Fields that are automatically filled by the system in response to events, like "Signing completion date". |
| `system_computed` | Fields that are calculated by the system based on other metadata values. e.g. "Signing duration" is the difference between "Signing date" and "Signed date"
| `system` | Other system-provided metadata fields. |

Note that the `type` is distinct from the `value_type`, which controls which kinds of values may be stored in the
field.
